title: Chocolate fountain
body:
A chocolate fountain is a device for serving chocolate fondue. Typical examples resemble a stepped cone, standing 2–4 feet (0.61–1.22 m) tall with a crown at the top and stacked tiers over a basin at the bottom. The basin is heated to keep the chocolate in a liquid state so it can be pulled into a center cylinder then vertically transported to the top of the fountain by an Archimedes screw. From there it flows over the tiers creating a chocolate "waterfall" in which food items like strawberries or marshmallows can be dipped.

The Wenschitz Confiserie in Allhaming, Austria was the world's tallest chocolate fountain, being 12.27 metres (40.3 ft) tall.
categories: Articles needing additional references from March 2007
